   474→#' Column Contrast Specification
   475→#'
   476→#' @description
   477→#' Define a contrast by directly targeting design matrix columns using regex patterns.
   478→#' This is useful for contrasts involving continuous variables or specific basis functions.
   479→#'
   480→#' @param pattern_A A character string containing a regex pattern to identify the
   481→#'   columns for the positive (+) part of the contrast.
   482→#' @param pattern_B Optional character string containing a regex pattern for the
   483→#'   negative (-) part (for A-B type contrasts). If NULL, creates a contrast testing
   484→#'   the average of columns matching `pattern_A` against baseline (0).
   485→#' @param name A character string name for the contrast (mandatory).
   486→#' @param where Currently unused for column_contrast, but kept for API consistency.
   487→#'
   488→#' @return A `column_contrast_spec` object containing the specification.
   489→#'
   490→#' @details
   491→#' This contrast type operates by finding design matrix columns whose names match
   492→#' the provided patterns (`pattern_A`, `pattern_B`). It calculates weights such that
   493→#' the average effect of the 'A' columns is compared to the average effect of the
   494→#' 'B' columns (or baseline if `pattern_B` is NULL). Weights are assigned as +1/nA
   495→#' for 'A' columns and -1/nB for 'B' columns, ensuring the contrast sums to zero
   496→#' if both A and B groups are present.
   497→#'
   498→#' Use standard R regex syntax for the patterns. Remember to escape special
   499→#' characters (e.g., `\\[`, `\\.`, `\\*`).
   500→#'
   501→#' @examples
   502→#' # Test the main effect of a continuous modulator 'RT'
   503→#' # Assumes RT is a column name, e.g., from columns(Scale(RT))
   504→#' cc1 <- column_contrast(pattern_A = "^z_RT$", name = "Main_RT")
   505→#'
   506→#' # Compare Condition.A vs Condition.B for the 'RT' modulator effect
   507→#' # Assumes condition names like "Condition.A_z_RT", "Condition.B_z_RT"
   508→#' cc2 <- column_contrast(pattern_A = "^Condition\\.A_z_RT$",
   509→#'                        pattern_B = "^Condition\\.B_z_RT$",
   510→#'                        name = "CondA_vs_CondB_for_RT")
   511→#'
   512→#' # Test a specific basis function (e.g., basis spline #3)
   513→#' # Assumes column names like "TermName_Condition.Tag_b03"
   514→#' cc3 <- column_contrast(pattern_A = "_b03$", name = "Basis_3_Effect")
   515→#'
   516→#' @export
   517→column_contrast <- function(pattern_A, pattern_B = NULL, name, where = NULL) {
   518→  # Input validation
   519→  assert_that(is.character(pattern_A) && length(pattern_A) == 1,
   520→              msg = "pattern_A must be a single character string")
   521→  if (!is.null(pattern_B)) {
   522→    assert_that(is.character(pattern_B) && length(pattern_B) == 1,
   523→                msg = "pattern_B must be a single character string")
   524→  }
   525→  assert_that(is.character(name) && length(name) == 1,
   526→              msg = "name must be a single character string")
   527→  if (!is.null(where)) {
   528→      warning("'where' argument is currently ignored for column_contrast.")
   529→      # assert_that(rlang::is_formula(where)) # Keep structure if needed later
   530→  }
   531→
   532→  ret <- list(
   533→    pattern_A = pattern_A,
   534→    pattern_B = pattern_B,
   535→    where = where, # Store it even if unused for now
   536→    name = name
   537→  )
   538→
   539→  class(ret) <- c("column_contrast_spec", "contrast_spec", "list")
   540→  ret
   541→}
   542→
   543→#' Polynomial Contrast
   544→#'
   545→#' @description
   546→#' Create polynomial contrasts for testing trends across ordered factor levels. This is
   547→#' particularly useful for analyzing factors with a natural ordering (e.g., time, dose).
   548→#'
   549→#' @param A A formula specifying the ordered factor.
   550→#' @param name A character string identifying the contrast.
   551→#' @param where An optional formula for subsetting the data.
   552→#' @param degree An integer specifying the degree of the polynomial (default: 1).
   553→#' @param value_map An optional list mapping factor levels to numeric values.
   554→#'
   555→#' @return A poly_contrast_spec object containing the specification for generating
   556→#'   polynomial contrast weights.
   557→#'
   558→#' @details
   559→#' The function creates orthogonal polynomial contrasts up to the specified degree.
   560→#' These contrasts can test for linear, quadratic, cubic, and higher-order trends
   561→#' in the data. The value_map parameter allows for non-uniform spacing between levels.
   562→#'
   563→#' @examples
   564→#' # Linear trend across time points
   565→#' pcon <- poly_contrast(~ time, name = "linear_time", degree = 1)
   566→#'
   567→#' # Cubic trend with custom spacing
   568→#' pcon <- poly_contrast(~ dose, name = "dose_cubic",
   569→#'                      degree = 3,
   570→#'                      value_map = list("low" = 0, "med" = 2, "high" = 5))
   571→#'
   572→#' @seealso
   573→#' \code{\link{oneway_contrast}} for categorical contrasts,
   574→#' \code{\link{interaction_contrast}} for interaction effects
   575→#'
   576→#' @export
   577→poly_contrast <- function(A, name, where = NULL, degree = 1, value_map = NULL) {
   578→  # Input validation
   579→  assert_that(rlang::is_formula(A),
   580→              msg = "A must be a formula")
   581→  assert_that(is.character(name) && length(name) == 1,
   582→              msg = "name must be a single character string")
   583→  assert_that(is.numeric(degree) && length(degree) == 1 && degree >= 1,
   584→              msg = "degree must be a positive integer")
   585→  
   586→  if (!is.null(where)) {
   587→    assert_that(rlang::is_formula(where),
   588→                msg = "where must be a formula")
   589→  }
   590→  if (!is.null(value_map)) {
   591→    assert_that(is.list(value_map),
   592→                msg = "value_map must be a list")
   593→  }
   594→
   595→  ret <- list(
   596→    A=A,
   597→    B=NULL,
   598→    where=where,
   599→    degree=degree,
   600→    value_map=value_map,
   601→    name=name)
   602→  
   603→  class(ret) <- c("poly_contrast_spec", "contrast_spec", "list")
   604→  ret
   605→}
   606→
   607→#' Unit Contrast Weights
   608→#'
   609→#' @description
   610→#' Compute the contrast weights for a unit_contrast_spec object.
   611→#'
   612→#' @param x A unit_contrast_spec object.
   613→#' @param term A term object.
   614→#' @param ... Additional arguments (currently unused).
   615→#'
   616→#' @return A list containing the term, name, weights, condition names, and contrast specification.
   617→#'
   618→#' @export
   619→contrast_weights.unit_contrast_spec <- function(x, term,...) {
   620→  # All possible condition names (pre-basis expansion)
   621→  all_condnames <- try(conditions(term, drop.empty = FALSE, expand_basis = FALSE), silent = TRUE)
   622→  if (inherits(all_condnames, "try-error") || length(all_condnames) == 0) {
   623→      warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Failed to get condition names for term '", term$varname, "'."), call. = FALSE)
   624→      all_condnames <- character(0)
   625→  }
   626→
   627→  weights_out <- matrix(0, nrow = length(all_condnames), ncol = 1)
   628→  rownames(weights_out) <- all_condnames
   629→  colnames(weights_out) <- x$name
   630→
   631→  term_cells <- cells(term)
   632→  if (nrow(term_cells) > 0 && length(all_condnames) > 0) {
   633→      # Apply 'where' clause
   634→      keep <- if (!is.null(x$where)) {
   635→        tryCatch(rlang::eval_tidy(rlang::f_rhs(x$where), data = term_cells), error = function(e) {
   636→            warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Error evaluating 'where' clause: ", e$message), call. = FALSE)
   637→            rep(FALSE, nrow(term_cells))
   638→        })
   639→      } else {
   640→        rep(TRUE, nrow(term_cells))
   641→      }
   642→
   643→      relevant_cells <- term_cells[keep, , drop = FALSE]
   644→
   645→      if (nrow(relevant_cells) == 0) {
   646→          warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "' resulted in no relevant cells after applying the 'where' clause."), call. = FALSE)
   647→      } else {
   648→          # Map relevant cells to sanitized condition names
   649→          term_vars <- names(relevant_cells)
   650→          token_df <- Map(function(col, nm) level_token(nm, col), relevant_cells, term_vars)
   651→          token_df <- as.data.frame(token_df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
   652→          target_cond_names <- apply(token_df, 1, make_cond_tag)
   653→          idx <- match(target_cond_names, all_condnames)
   654→
   655→          mask_A_full <- logical(length(all_condnames))
   656→          mask_A_full[idx[!is.na(idx)]] <- TRUE
   657→
   658→          weights_named <- .calculate_mask_weights(all_condnames, mask_A_full)
   659→          weights_out[,1] <- weights_named
   660→      }
   661→  }
   662→
   663→  # Return structure focused on cell-based weights
   664→  ret <- list(
   665→    term=term,
   666→    name=x$name,
   667→    weights=weights_out,
   668→    condnames=all_condnames,
   669→    contrast_spec=x
   670→  )
   671→  
   672→  class(ret) <- c("unit_contrast", "cell_contrast", "contrast", "list")
   673→  ret
   674→}
   675→
   676→
   677→#' @export
   678→`-.contrast_spec` <- function(e1, e2, ...){
   679→  assert_that(inherits(e2, "contrast_spec"))
   680→  structure(list(
   681→    name=paste0(e1$name, ":", e2$name),
   682→    con1=e1,
   683→    con2=e2),
   684→    class=c("contrast_diff_spec", "contrast_spec", "list")
   685→  )
   686→}
   687→
   688→
   689→#' One-way Contrast Weights
   690→#'
   691→#' @description
   692→#' Compute the contrast weights for an oneway_contrast_spec object.
   693→#'
   694→#' @param x An oneway_contrast_spec object.
   695→#' @param term A term object.
   696→#' @param ... Additional arguments (currently unused).
   697→#'
   698→#' @return A list containing the term, name, weights, condition names, and contrast specification.
   699→#'
   700→#' @export
   701→contrast_weights.oneway_contrast_spec <- function(x, term,...) {
   702→  # Get cells (categorical only)
   703→  term_cells <- cells(term)
   704→  if (nrow(term_cells) == 0) {
   705→       warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Term '", term$varname, "' has no categorical cells."), call. = FALSE)
   706→       weights_out <- matrix(numeric(0), nrow = 0, ncol = 0) # F-contrast, ncol unknown yet
   707→       cell_names_out <- character(0)
   708→  } else { 
   709→      # Apply 'where' clause
   710→      keep <- if (!is.null(x$where)) {
   711→        tryCatch(rlang::eval_tidy(rlang::f_rhs(x$where), data = term_cells), error = function(e) {
   712→            warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Error evaluating 'where' clause: ", e$message), call. = FALSE)
   713→            rep(FALSE, nrow(term_cells))
   714→        })
   715→      } else {
   716→        rep(TRUE, nrow(term_cells))
   717→      }
   718→      
   719→      relevant_cells <- term_cells[keep, , drop = FALSE]
   720→      
   721→      if (nrow(relevant_cells) == 0) {
   722→          warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "' resulted in no relevant cells after applying the 'where' clause."), call. = FALSE)
   723→          weights_out <- matrix(numeric(0), nrow = 0, ncol = 0)
   724→          cell_names_out <- character(0)
   725→      } else {
   726→          # Identify the factor for the main effect
   727→          fac_name <- all.vars(rlang::f_rhs(x$A))
   728→          if (length(fac_name) > 1) {
   729→              warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': one-way contrast has >1 factor specified (", 
   730→                            paste(fac_name, collapse=", "), "), using first: ", fac_name[1]), call.=FALSE)
   731→              fac_name <- fac_name[1]
   732→          }
   733→          if (!(fac_name %in% names(relevant_cells))){
   734→               stop(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': factor ", fac_name, " not found in relevant cells."), call.=FALSE)
   735→          }
   736→          
   737→          # Generate contrast matrix relative to the levels of the factor in relevant_cells
   738→          # Assuming generate_main_effect_contrast works on the cell structure
   739→          # It should return a matrix where rows correspond to relevant_cells
   740→          cmat <- tryCatch(generate_main_effect_contrast(relevant_cells, fac_name), error = function(e) {
   741→              stop(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Error generating main effect contrast for factor ", fac_name, ": ", e$message), call.=FALSE)
   742→          })
   743→          
   744→          # Ensure rownames match cell identifiers (if helper doesn't add them)
   745→          # Create unique names/identifiers for the relevant cells 
   746→          cell_names_rel <- apply(relevant_cells, 1, paste, collapse = "_")
   747→          rownames(cmat) <- cell_names_rel
   748→          colnames(cmat) <- paste(x$name, seq_len(ncol(cmat)), sep="_") # Name F-contrast columns
   749→          
   750→          weights_out <- cmat
   751→          cell_names_out <- cell_names_rel
   752→      }
   753→  }
   754→
   755→  # Return structure focused on cell-based weights
   756→  ret <- list(
   757→    term = term,
   758→    name = x$name,
   759→    weights = weights_out, # Weights matrix relative to relevant cells
   760→    condnames = cell_names_out, # Names of relevant cells
   761→    contrast_spec = x
   762→  )
   763→  
   764→  # Classify as Fcontrast if multiple columns, otherwise simple contrast
   765→  base_class <- if(ncol(weights_out) > 1) "Fcontrast" else "contrast"
   766→  class(ret) <- c("oneway_contrast", base_class, "cell_contrast", "contrast", "list")
   767→  ret
   768→}
   769→
   770→#' Interaction Contrast Weights
   771→#'
   772→#' @description
   773→#' Compute the contrast weights for an interaction_contrast_spec object.
   774→#'
   775→#' @param x An interaction_contrast_spec object.
   776→#' @param term A term object.
   777→#' @param ... Additional arguments (currently unused).
   778→#'
   779→#' @return A list containing the term, name, weights, condition names, and contrast specification.
   780→#'
   781→#' @export
   782→contrast_weights.interaction_contrast_spec <- function(x, term,...) {
   783→  # Get cells (categorical only)
   784→  term_cells <- cells(term)
   785→  if (nrow(term_cells) == 0) {
   786→       warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Term '", term$varname, "' has no categorical cells for interaction."), call. = FALSE)
   787→       weights_out <- matrix(numeric(0), nrow = 0, ncol = 0)
   788→       cell_names_out <- character(0)
   789→  } else {
   790→      # Apply 'where' clause
   791→      keep <- if (!is.null(x$where)) {
   792→        tryCatch(rlang::eval_tidy(rlang::f_rhs(x$where), data = term_cells), error = function(e) {
   793→            warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Error evaluating 'where' clause: ", e$message), call. = FALSE)
   794→            rep(FALSE, nrow(term_cells))
   795→        })
   796→      } else {
   797→        rep(TRUE, nrow(term_cells))
   798→      }
   799→      
   800→      relevant_cells <- term_cells[keep, , drop = FALSE]
   801→      
   802→      if (nrow(relevant_cells) == 0) {
   803→          warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "' resulted in no relevant cells after applying the 'where' clause."), call. = FALSE)
   804→          weights_out <- matrix(numeric(0), nrow = 0, ncol = 0)
   805→          cell_names_out <- character(0)
   806→      } else {
   807→          # Identify factors for interaction
   808→          factors <- all.vars(rlang::f_rhs(x$A))
   809→          if (length(factors) < 2) {
   810→              stop(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Interaction contrast requires at least two factors."), call.=FALSE)
   811→          }
   812→          if (!all(factors %in% names(relevant_cells))) {
   813→              missing_facs <- factors[!factors %in% names(relevant_cells)]
   814→              stop(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Factor(s)", paste(missing_facs, collapse=", "), "not found in relevant cells."), call.=FALSE)
   815→          }
   816→          
   817→          # Generate interaction contrast matrix relative to relevant cells
   818→          # Assuming generate_interaction_contrast works on cell structure
   819→          cmat <- tryCatch(generate_interaction_contrast(relevant_cells, factors), error = function(e) {
   820→              stop(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Error generating interaction contrast: ", e$message), call.=FALSE)
   821→          })
   822→          
   823→          # Ensure rownames match cell identifiers
   824→          cell_names_rel <- apply(relevant_cells, 1, paste, collapse = "_")
   825→          rownames(cmat) <- cell_names_rel
   826→          colnames(cmat) <- paste(x$name, seq_len(ncol(cmat)), sep="_") # Name F-contrast columns
   827→          
   828→          weights_out <- cmat
   829→          cell_names_out <- cell_names_rel
   830→      }
   831→  }
   832→  
   833→  # Return structure focused on cell-based weights
   834→  ret <- list(
   835→    term = term,
   836→    name = x$name,
   837→    weights = weights_out, # Weights matrix relative to relevant cells
   838→    condnames = cell_names_out, # Names of relevant cells
   839→    contrast_spec = x
   840→  )
   841→  
   842→  # Classify as Fcontrast (interactions usually are)
   843→  base_class <- if(is.null(ncol(weights_out)) || ncol(weights_out) > 1) "Fcontrast" else "contrast"
   844→  class(ret) <- c("interaction_contrast", base_class, "cell_contrast", "contrast", "list")
   845→  ret
   846→}
   847→
   848→#' Polynomial Contrast Weights
   849→#'
   850→#' @description
   851→#' Compute the contrast weights for a poly_contrast_spec object.
   852→#'
   853→#' @param x A poly_contrast_spec object.
   854→#' @param term A term object.
   855→#' @param ... Additional arguments (currently unused).
   856→#'
   857→#' @return A list containing the term, name, weights, condition names, and contrast specification.
   858→#'
   859→#' @export
   860→contrast_weights.poly_contrast_spec <- function(x, term,...) {
   861→  all_condnames <- try(conditions(term, drop.empty = FALSE, expand_basis = FALSE), silent = TRUE)
   862→  if (inherits(all_condnames, "try-error") || length(all_condnames) == 0) {
   863→      warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Failed to get condition names for term '", term$varname, "'."), call. = FALSE)
   864→      all_condnames <- character(0)
   865→  }
   866→
   867→  weights_out <- matrix(0, nrow = length(all_condnames), ncol = x$degree)
   868→  rownames(weights_out) <- all_condnames
   869→  colnames(weights_out) <- paste(x$name, 1:x$degree, sep="_")
   870→
   871→  term_cells <- cells(term)
   872→  if (nrow(term_cells) > 0 && length(all_condnames) > 0) {
   873→      keep <- if (!is.null(x$where)) {
   874→        tryCatch(rlang::eval_tidy(rlang::f_rhs(x$where), data = term_cells), error = function(e) {
   875→            warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Error evaluating 'where' clause: ", e$message), call. = FALSE)
   876→            rep(FALSE, nrow(term_cells))
   877→        })
   878→      } else {
   879→        rep(TRUE, nrow(term_cells))
   880→      }
   881→
   882→      relevant_cells <- term_cells[keep, , drop = FALSE]
   883→
   884→      if (nrow(relevant_cells) == 0) {
   885→          warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "' resulted in no relevant cells after applying the 'where' clause."), call. = FALSE)
   886→      } else {
   887→          vals_fac <- tryCatch(rlang::eval_tidy(rlang::f_rhs(x$A), data = relevant_cells), error = function(e) {
   888→              stop(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Error evaluating formula A: ", e$message), call.=FALSE)
   889→          })
   890→
   891→          vals_num <- if (is.null(x$value_map)) {
   892→              tryCatch(as.numeric(as.character(vals_fac)), warning = function(w){
   893→                  stop(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Cannot coerce factor levels from formula A to numeric for poly contrast. Use value_map? Error: ", w$message), call.=FALSE)
   894→              })
   895→          } else {
   896→              mapped_vals <- x$value_map[as.character(vals_fac)]
   897→              if(anyNA(mapped_vals) || length(mapped_vals) != length(vals_fac)){
   898→                  stop(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': value_map does not cover all factor levels present in relevant cells."), call.=FALSE)
   899→              }
   900→              unlist(mapped_vals)
   901→          }
   902→
   903→          if (length(unique(vals_num)) <= x$degree) {
   904→               stop(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Polynomial degree (", x$degree,
   905→                           ") is too high for the number of unique points (", length(unique(vals_num)), ") in relevant cells."), call.=FALSE)
   906→          }
   907→
   908→          pvals_mat <- tryCatch(stats::poly(vals_num, degree = x$degree), error = function(e){
   909→               stop(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Error calculating polynomial weights: ", e$message), call.=FALSE)
   910→          })
   911→
   912→          term_vars <- names(relevant_cells)
   913→          token_df <- Map(function(col, nm) level_token(nm, col), relevant_cells, term_vars)
   914→          token_df <- as.data.frame(token_df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
   915→          target_cond_names <- apply(token_df, 1, make_cond_tag)
   916→          idx <- match(target_cond_names, all_condnames)
   917→          valid_idx <- which(!is.na(idx))
   918→          if (length(valid_idx) > 0) {
   919→              weights_out[idx[valid_idx], ] <- pvals_mat[valid_idx, , drop = FALSE]
   920→          }
   921→      }
   922→  }
   923→
   924→  ret <- list(
   925→    term = term,
   926→    name = x$name,
   927→    weights = weights_out,
   928→    condnames = all_condnames,
   929→    contrast_spec = x
   930→  )
   931→  
   932→  # Classify as Fcontrast (poly usually has multiple columns)
   933→  base_class <- if(is.null(ncol(weights_out)) || ncol(weights_out) > 1) "Fcontrast" else "contrast"
   934→  class(ret) <- c("poly_contrast", base_class, "cell_contrast", "contrast", "list")
   935→  ret
   936→}
   937→
   938→#' Pair Contrast Weights
   939→#'
   940→#' @description
   941→#' Compute the contrast weights for a pair_contrast_spec object.
   942→#'
   943→#' @param x A pair_contrast_spec object.
   944→#' @param term A term object.
   945→#' @param ... Additional arguments (currently unused).
   946→#'
   947→#' @return A list containing the term, name, weights, condition names, and contrast specification.
   948→#'
   949→#' @export
   950→contrast_weights.pair_contrast_spec <- function(x, term,...) {
   951→  # Get cells (only categorical combinations) for evaluating formulas A & B
   952→  term_cells <- cells(term)
   953→  
   954→  # Determine if basis expansion is needed
   955→  expand_basis <- FALSE
   956→  nbasis <- 1L
   957→  hrfspec <- attr(term, "hrfspec")
   958→  if (!is.null(hrfspec) && !is.null(hrfspec$hrf)) {
   959→    hrf_fun <- hrfspec$hrf
   960→    # Check nbasis using the nbasis generic
   961→    if (!inherits(try(fmrihrf::nbasis(hrf_fun), silent=TRUE), "try-error") && fmrihrf::nbasis(hrf_fun) > 1) {
   962→       nbasis <- fmrihrf::nbasis(hrf_fun)
   963→       expand_basis <- TRUE
   964→    }
   965→  }
   966→  
   967→  # Get base condition names (pre-expansion) - these are the targets for weights
   968→  base_condnames_all <- try(conditions(term, drop.empty=FALSE, expand_basis=FALSE), silent=TRUE)
   969→  if (inherits(base_condnames_all, "try-error") || length(base_condnames_all) == 0) {
   970→      warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Failed to get base condition names for term '", term$varname, "'. Skipping."), call. = FALSE)
   971→      return(NULL) # Return NULL if cannot proceed
   972→  }
   973→  
   974→  if (nrow(term_cells) == 0) {
   975→      # If term_cells is empty, we can still proceed if base_condnames_all exist,
   976→      # but the contrast formulas A/B likely won't match anything.
   977→      warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Term '", term$varname, "' has no observed categorical cells. Contrast weights might be all zero."), call. = FALSE)
   978→  }
   979→  
   980→  # Evaluate 'where' clause on term_cells
   981→  keep <- if (!is.null(x$where)) {
   982→      tryCatch(rlang::eval_tidy(rlang::f_rhs(x$where), data = term_cells), error = function(e) {
   983→          warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Error evaluating 'where' clause: ", e$message), call. = FALSE)
   984→          rep(FALSE, nrow(term_cells)) # Default to FALSE on error
   985→      })
   986→  } else {
   987→      rep(TRUE, nrow(term_cells)) # Keep all if no 'where'
   988→  }
   989→  relevant_cells <- term_cells[keep, , drop = FALSE]
   990→  
   991→  if (nrow(relevant_cells) == 0 && nrow(term_cells) > 0) {
   992→       warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "' resulted in no relevant cells after applying the 'where' clause."), call. = FALSE)
   993→       # Proceed, but weights will likely be zero
   994→  }
   995→  
   996→  # Evaluate A and B formulas on the relevant cells
   997→  keepA_rel <- if(nrow(relevant_cells) > 0) tryCatch(rlang::eval_tidy(rlang::f_rhs(x$A), data = relevant_cells), error = function(e) {
   998→      warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Error evaluating formula A: ", e$message), call. = FALSE)
   999→      logical(nrow(relevant_cells)) # Return all FALSE on error
  1000→  }) else logical(0)
  1001→  
  1002→  keepB_rel <- if (is.null(x$B) || nrow(relevant_cells) == 0) NULL else {
  1003→      tryCatch(rlang::eval_tidy(rlang::f_rhs(x$B), data = relevant_cells), error = function(e) {
  1004→          warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Error evaluating formula B: ", e$message), call. = FALSE)
  1005→          logical(nrow(relevant_cells)) # Return all FALSE on error
  1006→      })
  1007→  }
  1008→  
  1009→  # --- FIX: Map formula evaluation results to condition names --- 
  1010→  mask_A_full <- logical(length(base_condnames_all))
  1011→  mask_B_full <- if (!is.null(keepB_rel)) logical(length(base_condnames_all)) else NULL
  1012→  
  1013→  if (nrow(relevant_cells) > 0) {
  1014→    # Vectorized construction of condition names for all relevant cells
  1015→    term_vars <- names(relevant_cells)
  1016→    token_df <- Map(function(col, nm) level_token(nm, col), relevant_cells, term_vars)
  1017→    token_df <- as.data.frame(token_df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  1018→    target_cond_names <- apply(token_df, 1, make_cond_tag)
  1019→
  1020→    idx_all <- match(target_cond_names, base_condnames_all)
  1021→
  1022→    len_A <- min(length(keepA_rel), length(idx_all))
  1023→    if (len_A > 0) {
  1024→      idx_A <- idx_all[seq_len(len_A)]
  1025→      mask_A_full[idx_A[keepA_rel[seq_len(len_A)] & !is.na(idx_A)]] <- TRUE
  1026→    }
  1027→
  1028→    if (!is.null(mask_B_full)) {
  1029→      len_B <- min(length(keepB_rel), length(idx_all))
  1030→      if (len_B > 0) {
  1031→        idx_B <- idx_all[seq_len(len_B)]
  1032→        mask_B_full[idx_B[keepB_rel[seq_len(len_B)] & !is.na(idx_B)]] <- TRUE
  1033→      }
  1034→    }
  1035→  }
  1036→  # --- END FIX --- 
  1037→
  1038→  # Calculate base weights relative to the full set of base conditions
  1039→  base_weights_named <- .calculate_mask_weights(base_condnames_all, mask_A_full, mask_B_full)
  1040→  
  1041→  # --- Basis Expansion --- 
  1042→  if (expand_basis) {
  1043→     # Get expanded condition names
  1044→     expanded_condnames <- try(conditions(term, drop.empty = FALSE, expand_basis = TRUE), silent = TRUE)
  1045→     if (inherits(expanded_condnames, "try-error") || length(expanded_condnames) == 0) {
  1046→         warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Failed to get expanded condition names for term '", term$varname, "'. Skipping expansion."), call. = FALSE)
  1047→         weights_out <- matrix(base_weights_named, ncol = 1)
  1048→         rownames(weights_out) <- names(base_weights_named)
  1049→         colnames(weights_out) <- x$name
  1050→         cell_names_out <- names(base_weights_named)
  1051→     } else if (length(expanded_condnames) != length(base_weights_named) * nbasis) {
  1052→          warning(paste("Contrast '", x$name, "': Mismatch between expanded names (", length(expanded_condnames), 
  1053→                        ") and expected from base weights * nbasis (", length(base_weights_named) * nbasis, "). Skipping expansion."), call. = FALSE)
  1054→          weights_out <- matrix(base_weights_named, ncol = 1)
  1055→          rownames(weights_out) <- names(base_weights_named)
  1056→          colnames(weights_out) <- x$name
  1057→          cell_names_out <- names(base_weights_named)
  1058→     } else {
  1059→         # --- REMOVED MANUAL BROADCAST ---
  1060→         # weights_out_vec <- rep(base_weights_named, each = nbasis)
  1061→         # --- Instead, align using names ---
  1062→         weights_out <- matrix(0, nrow = length(expanded_condnames), ncol = 1)
  1063→         rownames(weights_out) <- expanded_condnames
  1064→         colnames(weights_out) <- x$name
  1065→         # Find expanded names corresponding to non-zero base weights
  1066→         base_names_with_weight <- names(base_weights_named)[base_weights_named != 0]
  1067→         # Create regex patterns to match expanded names
  1068→         # patterns_to_match <- paste0("^", base_names_with_weight, "(\_b\\d+)?$") # Match name + optional _b suffix
  1069→         
  1070→         for(i in seq_along(base_names_with_weight)){
  1071→             base_name <- base_names_with_weight[i]
  1072→             weight_val <- base_weights_named[base_name]
  1073→             # Find all expanded conditions that start with this base name
  1074→             # Use fixed=TRUE for exact match of base name part
  1075→             pattern <- paste0("^", base_name, "(_b\\d+)?$")   # note: _b\\d+
  1076→             matching_expanded_indices <- grep(pattern, expanded_condnames, perl = TRUE)
  1077→            if(length(matching_expanded_indices) > 0){
  1078→                 weights_out[matching_expanded_indices, 1] <- weight_val 
  1079→             }
  1080→         }
  1081→         # Ensure weights still sum to zero (or close to it)
  1082→         if (abs(sum(weights_out)) > .CONTRAST_TOLERANCE) {
  1083→              warning(paste("Contrast ", x$name, ": Weights do not sum to zero after basis expansion."), call.=FALSE)
  1084→         }
  1085→         cell_names_out <- expanded_condnames
  1086→     }
  1087→  } else {
  1088→     # No expansion needed
  1089→     weights_out <- matrix(base_weights_named, ncol = 1)
  1090→     rownames(weights_out) <- names(base_weights_named)
  1091→     colnames(weights_out) <- x$name
  1092→     cell_names_out <- names(base_weights_named)
  1093→  }
  1094→
  1095→  # Return structure focused on cell-based weights
  1096→  ret <- list(
  1097→    term = term,
  1098→    name = x$name,
  1099→    weights = weights_out, # Weights relative to (potentially expanded) relevant cells
  1100→    condnames = cell_names_out, # Names of the relevant cells corresponding to rows of weights
  1101→    contrast_spec = x
  1102→  )
  1103→  
  1104→  class(ret) <- c("cell_contrast", "contrast", "list") 
  1105→  ret  
  1106→}
  1107→
  1108→#' Column Contrast Weights
  1109→#'
  1110→#' @description
  1111→#' Compute contrast weights for a `column_contrast_spec` object by targeting
  1112→#' design matrix columns based on regex patterns.
  1113→#'
  1114→#' @param x A `column_contrast_spec` object.
  1115→#' @param term An `event_term` object.
  1116→#' @param ... Additional arguments (currently unused).
  1117→#'
  1118→#' @return A list containing the contrast details:
  1119→#'   \item{term}{The original `event_term` object.}
  1120→#'   \item{name}{The name of the contrast.}
  1121→#'   \item{weights}{A numeric matrix where rows correspond to the full design
  1122→#'                  matrix columns (from `.condnames(term, expanded = TRUE)`)
  1123→#'                  and columns represent the contrast(s). Usually one column.}
  1124→#'   \item{condnames}{Character vector of all potential *expanded* condition names from `term`.}
  1125→#'   \item{contrast_spec}{The original `column_contrast_spec` object.}
  1126→#'
  1127→#' @export
  1128→#' @import assertthat
  1129→contrast_weights.column_contrast_spec <- function(x, term, ...) {
  1130→
  1131→  # --- Use .condnames helper to get expanded names --- 
  1132→  all_colnames <- .condnames(term, expanded = TRUE)
  1133→  # Note: .condnames() already includes error handling for conditions()
  1134→  if (length(all_colnames) == 0) {
  1135→      # It's possible conditions() returns empty if term has no levels/columns
  1136→      warning(paste("Column contrast '", x$name, "': Term '", term$varname %||% "<unknown>",
  1137→                    "' resulted in zero condition names. Weights will be empty."), call. = FALSE)
  1138→      # Return structure with empty weights
  1139→      weights_out <- matrix(numeric(0), nrow = 0, ncol = 1)
  1140→      colnames(weights_out) <- x$name
  1141→      ret <- list(
  1142→          term = term,
  1143→          name = x$name,
  1144→          weights = weights_out,
  1145→          condnames = character(0),
  1146→          contrast_spec = x
  1147→      )
  1148→      class(ret) <- c("column_contrast", "contrast", "list")
  1149→      return(ret)
  1150→  }
  1151→  num_all_conds <- length(all_colnames)
  1152→
  1153→  # --- Find indices using grep directly --- 
  1154→  # No need for .col_index intermediary anymore if we have names
  1155→  idx_A <- grep(x$pattern_A, all_colnames, value = FALSE)
  1156→  nA <- length(idx_A)
  1157→  if (nA == 0) {
  1158→    warning(paste("Column contrast '", x$name, "': pattern_A ('", x$pattern_A,
  1159→                  "') did not match any design matrix columns for term '", term$varname %||% "<unknown>", "'."),
  1160→            call. = FALSE)
  1161→  }
  1162→
  1163→  idx_B <- integer(0)
  1164→  nB <- 0
  1165→  if (!is.null(x$pattern_B)) {
  1166→    idx_B <- grep(x$pattern_B, all_colnames, value = FALSE)
  1167→    nB <- length(idx_B)
  1168→    if (nB == 0) {
  1169→      warning(paste("Column contrast '", x$name, "': pattern_B ('", x$pattern_B,
  1170→                    "') did not match any design matrix columns for term '", term$varname %||% "<unknown>", "'."),
  1171→              call. = FALSE)
  1172→    }
  1173→  }
  1174→
  1175→  # --- Retain overlap check --- 
  1176→  if (nA > 0 && nB > 0 && any(idx_A %in% idx_B)) {
  1177→    stop(paste("Column contrast '", x$name, "': pattern_A and pattern_B match overlapping columns.",
  1178→               " Indices A: ", paste(idx_A, collapse=", "),
  1179→               "; Indices B: ", paste(idx_B, collapse=", ")), 
  1180→         call. = FALSE)
  1181→  }
  1182→
  1183→  # --- Calculate weights using weight helper --- 
  1184→  mask_A <- seq_along(all_colnames) %in% idx_A
  1185→  mask_B <- if (!is.null(x$pattern_B)) seq_along(all_colnames) %in% idx_B else NULL
  1186→  
  1187→  # .calculate_mask_weights handles 1/nA, -1/nB, checks, and warnings
  1188→  weights_vec <- .calculate_mask_weights(all_colnames, mask_A, mask_B)
  1189→  
  1190→  # Ensure output is a matrix
  1191→  weights_mat <- matrix(weights_vec, ncol = 1)
  1192→  rownames(weights_mat) <- all_colnames
  1193→  colnames(weights_mat) <- x$name
  1194→
  1195→  # Return structure
  1196→  ret <- list(
  1197→    term = term,
  1198→    name = x$name,
  1199→    weights = weights_mat,
  1200→    condnames = all_colnames, # These are the expanded names used for weights
  1201→    contrast_spec = x
  1202→  )
  1203→
  1204→  # Classify appropriately
  1205→  class(ret) <- c("column_contrast", "contrast", "list")
  1206→  ret
  1207→}
  1208→
  1209→#' Contrast Formula Weights
  1210→#'
  1211→#' @description
  1212→#' Compute the contrast weights for a contrast_formula_spec object.
  1213→#'
  1214→#' @param x A contrast_formula_spec object.
  1215→#' @param term A term object.
  1216→#' @param ... Additional arguments (currently unused).
  1217→#'
  1218→#' @return A list containing the term, name, weights, condition names, and contrast specification.
  1219→#'
  1220→#' @export
  1221→contrast_weights.contrast_formula_spec <- function(x, term,...) {
  1222→
  1223→  term.cells <- cells(term)
  1224→  condnames <- shortnames(term)
  1225→  count <- attr(term.cells, "count")		
  1226→  term.cells <- subset(term.cells, count > 0)
  1227→  
  1228→  if (!is.null(x$where)) {
  1229→    keep <- rlang::eval_tidy(rlang::f_rhs(x$where), data=term.cells)
  1230→    assert_that(sum(keep) > 0)
  1231→    term.cells <- term.cells[keep,]
  1232→  } else {
  1233→    keep <- rep(TRUE, nrow(term.cells))
  1234→  }
  1235→  
  1236→  if (is_continuous(term)) {
  1237→    ## hack to handle contrasts with continuous terms
  1238→    facs <- !sapply(term$events, is_continuous)
  1239→    term.cells[,!facs] <- 1
  1240→  } 
  1241→
  1242→  # Create a simple environment with shortnames as variables
  1243→  # Each shortname gets a vector with 1 in its position and 0 elsewhere
  1244→  weights <- matrix(0, NROW(term.cells), 1)
  1245→  eval_env <- new.env(parent = rlang::f_env(x$A))
  1246→  
  1247→  # Create indicator variables for each condition using shortnames
  1248→  for (i in seq_along(condnames)) {
  1249→    indicator <- rep(0, length(condnames))
  1250→    indicator[i] <- 1
  1251→    assign(condnames[i], indicator, envir = eval_env)
  1252→  }
  1253→  
  1254→  # Evaluate the contrast formula
  1255→  res <- tryCatch(rlang::eval_tidy(rlang::f_rhs(x$A), env=eval_env), error = function(e) {
  1256→       stop(paste("Contrast formula evaluation failed:", e$message, "\nAvailable names:", paste(condnames, collapse=", ")), call.=FALSE)
  1257→  })
  1258→  
  1259→  # Apply results to weights matrix
  1260→  weights[keep,1] <- as.vector(res)
  1261→  
  1262→  # Use longnames for rownames (the new format)
  1263→  row.names(weights) <- longnames(term)[keep]
  1264→
  1265→  # Return structure
  1266→  ret <- list(
  1267→    term=term,
  1268→    name=x$name,
  1269→    weights=weights,
  1270→    condnames=longnames(term),
  1271→    contrast_spec=x)
  1272→  
  1273→  class(ret) <- c("contrast", "list")
  1274→  ret  
  1275→}
  1276→
  1277→#' Contrast Difference Weights
  1278→#'
  1279→#' @description
  1280→#' Compute the contrast weights for a contrast_diff_spec object.
  1281→#'
  1282→#' @param x A contrast_diff_spec object.
  1283→#' @param term A term object.
  1284→#' @param ... Additional arguments (currently unused).
  1285→#'
  1286→#' @return A list containing the term, name, weights, condition names, and contrast specification.
  1287→#'
  1288→#' @export
  1289→contrast_weights.contrast_diff_spec <- function(x, term,...) {
  1290→  wts1 <- contrast_weights(x$con1, term)
  1291→  wts2 <- contrast_weights(x$con2, term)
  1292→
  1293→  ret <- structure(
  1294→    list(
  1295→      term=term,
  1296→      name=x$name,
  1297→      weights=wts1$weights - wts2$weights,
  1298→      condnames=longnames(term),
  1299→      contrast_spec=x),
  1300→    class=c("contrast_diff", "contrast")
  1301→  )
  1302→
  1303→  ret  
  1304→}

  1306→#' Convert Contrast to GLT
  1307→#'
  1308→#' @description
  1309→#' Convert a contrast to an AFNI 'GLT' format.
  1310→#'
  1311→#' @param x The contrast to convert.
  1312→#' @param ... Additional arguments (currently unused).
  1313→#'
  1314→#' @return A list containing the GLT string, name, and contrast specification.
  1315→#'
  1316→#' @export
  1317→to_glt.contrast <- function(x,...) {
  1318→  if (is.matrix(x$weights) && ncol(x$weights) > 1) {
  1319→    glts <- lapply(1:ncol(x$weights), function(i) {
  1320→      paste0(signif(x$weights[,i],4), "*", x$condnames, collapse=" ")
  1321→    })
  1322→    
  1323→    ret <- list(glt_str=glts,
  1324→                name=paste0("GLT_", x$name, "_", 1:ncol(x$weights)),
  1325→                con=x)
  1326→    
  1327→    class(ret) <- "glt_contrast_list"
  1328→    ret
  1329→  } else {
  1330→    glt <- paste0(signif(x$weights,4), "*", x$condnames, collapse=" ")
  1331→    ret <- list(glt_str=glt,
  1332→       name=paste0("GLT_", x$name),
  1333→       con=x)
  1334→  
  1335→    class(ret) <- "glt_contrast"
  1336→    ret
  1337→  }
  1338→}

  1394→#' Print Contrast Set
  1395→#'
  1396→#' @description
  1397→#' Print a contrast set.
  1398→#'
  1399→#' @param x The contrast set to print.
  1400→#' @param ... Additional arguments (currently unused).
  1401→#'
  1402→#' @export
  1403→#' @rdname print
  1404→print.contrast_set <- function(x, ...) {
  1405→  n_contrasts <- length(x)
  1406→  
  1407→  # Header
  1408→  cat("\n=== Contrast Set ===\n")
  1409→  
  1410→  # Summary
  1411→  cat("\n Overview:\n")
  1412→  cat("  * Number of contrasts:", n_contrasts, "\n")
  1413→  
  1414→  # Group contrasts by type
  1415→  types <- sapply(x, function(con) class(con)[1])
  1416→  type_table <- table(types)
  1417→  if (length(type_table) > 0) {
  1418→    cat("  * Types of contrasts:\n")
  1419→    for (type in names(type_table)) {
  1420→      cat("    -", type, ":", type_table[type], "\n")
  1421→    }
  1422→  }
  1423→  
  1424→  # List all contrasts
  1425→  cat("\n  Individual Contrasts:\n")
  1426→  for (i in seq_along(x)) {
  1427→    cat("\n[", i, "] ", x[[i]]$name, " (", class(x[[i]])[1], ")\n", sep="")
  1428→    cat("    Formula: ")
  1429→    if (!is.null(x[[i]]$A)) cat(deparse(x[[i]]$A))
  1430→    if (!is.null(x[[i]]$B)) cat(" vs ", deparse(x[[i]]$B))
  1431→    cat("\n")
  1432→    if (!is.null(x[[i]]$where)) {
  1433→      cat("    Subset: ", deparse(x[[i]]$where), "\n")
  1434→    }
  1435→  }
  1436→  
  1437→  cat("\n")
  1438→  invisible(x)
  1439→}

  1531→#' plot_contrasts.event_model
  1532→#'
  1533→#' @description
  1534→#' Produces a heatmap of all contrasts defined for an \code{event_model}.
  1535→#' Rows = each contrast (or column of an F-contrast), columns = each regressor in
  1536→#' the full design matrix, and the fill color = the contrast weight.
  1537→#'
  1538→#' @param x An \code{event_model} with (lazily) defined contrasts.
  1539→#' @param absolute_limits Logical; if \code{TRUE}, the color scale is fixed at (-1,1).
  1540→#'   If \code{FALSE}, the range is set to (min, max) of the weights.
  1541→#' @param rotate_x_text Logical; if \code{TRUE}, rotate x-axis labels for readability.
  1542→#' @param scale_mode Character; 'auto', 'diverging', or 'one_sided' color scaling.
  1543→#' @param coord_fixed Logical; if TRUE, use fixed aspect ratio.
  1544→#' @param ... Further arguments passed to \code{geom_tile}, e.g. \code{color="grey80"}.
  1545→#'
  1546→#' @return A \code{ggplot2} object (a heatmap).
  1547→#' @import ggplot2
  1548→#' @export
  1549→plot_contrasts.event_model <- function(
  1550→    x,
  1551→    absolute_limits = FALSE,
  1552→    rotate_x_text   = TRUE,
  1553→    scale_mode      = c("auto", "diverging", "one_sided"),
  1554→    coord_fixed     = TRUE,
  1555→    ...
  1556→) {
  1557→  # 1) Extract all the design-matrix column names
  1558→  dm <- design_matrix(x)
  1559→  regressor_names <- colnames(dm)
  1560→  
  1561→  # 2) Gather contrast weights (the nested list by term, then by contrast)
  1562→  cws <- contrast_weights(x)
  1563→  
  1564→  # Flatten everything into one big matrix of contrast weights
  1565→  big_mat  <- NULL
  1566→  rownames <- character(0)
  1567→  
  1568→  add_contrast_row <- function(vec, row_name) {
  1569→    if (is.null(big_mat)) {
  1570→      big_mat <<- matrix(vec, nrow = 1)
  1571→      colnames(big_mat) <<- regressor_names
  1572→      rownames <<- row_name
  1573→    } else {
  1574→      big_mat <<- rbind(big_mat, vec)
  1575→      rownames <<- c(rownames, row_name)
  1576→    }
  1577→  }
  1578→  
  1579→  for (term_nm in names(cws)) {
  1580→    term_level <- cws[[term_nm]]
  1581→    if (is.null(term_level)) next
  1582→    
  1583→    for (contrast_nm in names(term_level)) {
  1584→      cw_obj <- term_level[[contrast_nm]]
  1585→      # By default, we store offset_weights in cw_obj$offset_weights
  1586→      W <- cw_obj$offset_weights
  1587→      if (is.null(W)) next  # skip if no offset_weights
  1588→      
  1589→      # (#designCols x #contrastCols)
  1590→      ncols <- ncol(W)
  1591→      for (k in seq_len(ncols)) {
  1592→        this_col <- W[, k]
  1593→        if (ncols > 1) {
  1594→          row_label <- paste0(contrast_nm, "_component", k)
  1595→        } else {
  1596→          row_label <- contrast_nm
  1597→        }
  1598→        add_contrast_row(this_col, row_label)
  1599→      }
  1600→    }
  1601→  }
  1602→  
  1603→  if (is.null(big_mat)) {
  1604→    stop("No contrasts found in this event_model.")
  1605→  }
  1606→  
  1607→  rownames(big_mat) <- rownames
  1608→  
  1609→  # 3) Convert big_mat to a long data frame using modern base R approach
  1610→  # Create indices for row and column positions
  1611→  row_indices <- rep(seq_len(nrow(big_mat)), ncol(big_mat))
  1612→  col_indices <- rep(seq_len(ncol(big_mat)), each = nrow(big_mat))
  1613→  
  1614→  # Create the long format data frame
  1615→  df_long <- data.frame(
  1616→    ContrastName = rownames(big_mat)[row_indices],
  1617→    Regressor = colnames(big_mat)[col_indices],
  1618→    Weight = as.vector(big_mat),
  1619→    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  1620→  )
  1621→  
  1622→  # 4) Build the ggplot
  1623→  plt <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  1624→    df_long,
  1625→    ggplot2::aes(
  1626→      x    = ReorderFactor(Regressor),
  1627→      y    = ReorderFactor(ContrastName, reverse = TRUE),
  1628→      fill = Weight
  1629→    )
  1630→  ) +
  1631→    ggplot2::geom_tile(...)
  1632→  
  1633→  # Decide on color scale
  1634→  scale_mode <- match.arg(scale_mode)
  1635→  wmin <- min(df_long$Weight, na.rm = TRUE)
  1636→  wmax <- max(df_long$Weight, na.rm = TRUE)
  1637→  
  1638→  # If user set absolute_limits=TRUE, we might forcibly use [-1,1] or [0,1]
  1639→  # but let's allow scale_mode to override as well.
  1640→  if (scale_mode == "diverging") {
  1641→    # Diverging scale, centered on 0
  1642→    lim_low  <- if (absolute_limits) -1 else wmin
  1643→    lim_high <- if (absolute_limits)  1 else wmax
  1644→    midpt <- 0
  1645→    
  1646→    plt <- plt + ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient2(
  1647→      limits   = c(lim_low, lim_high),
  1648→      midpoint = midpt,
  1649→      low      = "blue",
  1650→      mid      = "white",
  1651→      high     = "red"
  1652→    )
  1653→    
  1654→  } else if (scale_mode == "one_sided") {
  1655→    # One-sided scale for 0..1 or 0..something
  1656→    lim_low  <- if (absolute_limits) 0 else wmin
  1657→    lim_high <- if (absolute_limits) 1 else wmax
  1658→    
  1659→    plt <- plt + ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient(
  1660→      limits = c(lim_low, lim_high),
  1661→      low    = "white",
  1662→      high   = "red"
  1663→    )
  1664→    
  1665→  } else {
  1666→    # scale_mode == "auto"
  1667→    # If we detect any negative weight, do diverging; otherwise do one-sided
  1668→    if (wmin < 0) {
  1669→      # diverging
  1670→      lim_low  <- if (absolute_limits) -1 else wmin
  1671→      lim_high <- if (absolute_limits)  1 else wmax
  1672→      plt <- plt + ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient2(
  1673→        limits   = c(lim_low, lim_high),
  1674→        midpoint = 0,
  1675→        low      = "blue",
  1676→        mid      = "white",
  1677→        high     = "red"
  1678→      )
  1679→    } else {
  1680→      # one-sided
  1681→      lim_low  <- if (absolute_limits) 0 else wmin
  1682→      lim_high <- if (absolute_limits) 1 else wmax
  1683→      plt <- plt + ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient(
  1684→        limits = c(lim_low, lim_high),
  1685→        low    = "white",
  1686→        high   = "red"
  1687→      )
  1688→    }
  1689→  }
  1690→  
  1691→  # 5) Theming
  1692→  plt <- plt +
  1693→    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  1694→    ggplot2::labs(
  1695→      x    = "Regressor",
  1696→      y    = "Contrast",
  1697→      fill = "Weight"
  1698→    ) +
  1699→    ggplot2::theme(
  1700→      panel.grid  = ggplot2::element_blank(),
  1701→      axis.ticks  = ggplot2::element_blank()
  1702→    )
  1703→  
  1704→  if (rotate_x_text) {
  1705→    plt <- plt + ggplot2::theme(axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  1706→  }
  1707→  
  1708→  # 6) Optionally fix the coordinate ratio to keep tiles square
  1709→  if (coord_fixed) {
  1710→    plt <- plt +
  1711→      ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  1712→      ggplot2::scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  1713→      ggplot2::coord_fixed()
  1714→  }
  1715→  
  1716→  plt
  1717→}
  1718→
  1719→#' A small utility to preserve factor order in ggplot
  1720→#' 
  1721→#' Makes a factor from a character vector but preserves the order of appearance.
  1722→#' If `reverse=TRUE`, it reverses that order.
  1723→#' @keywords internal
  1724→#' @noRd
  1725→ReorderFactor <- function(x, reverse=FALSE) {
  1726→  levs <- unique(as.character(x))
  1727→  if (reverse) levs <- rev(levs)
  1728→  factor(x, levels=levs)
  1729→}
  1730→
  1731→#' Contrast Weights for a Contrast Set
  1732→#'
  1733→#' @description
  1734→#' Compute the contrast weights for each contrast specification within a contrast_set object.
  1735→#'
  1736→#' @param x A contrast_set object (a list of contrast_spec objects).
  1737→#' @param term A term object against which weights should be computed.
  1738→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to individual contrast_weights methods.
  1739→#'
  1740→#' @return A named list where each element is the result of calling contrast_weights 
  1741→#'         on the corresponding contrast_spec in the set. The list names are the 
  1742→#'         names of the individual contrasts.
  1743→#'
  1744→#' @export
  1745→#' @importFrom purrr map set_names
  1746→contrast_weights.contrast_set <- function(x, term, ...) {
  1747→  # Ensure x is a list (contrast_set inherits from list)
  1748→  if (!is.list(x)) {
  1749→    stop("Input 'x' must be a contrast_set (list).")
  1750→  }
  1751→  
  1752→  # Iterate through each contrast spec in the set
  1753→  results_list <- purrr::map(x, function(contrast_spec) {
  1754→    # Check if the element is actually a contrast_spec
  1755→    if (!inherits(contrast_spec, "contrast_spec")) {
  1756→      warning(paste("Element", contrast_spec$name, "is not a contrast_spec object, skipping."))
  1757→      return(NULL)
  1758→    }
  1759→    # Compute weights for the individual contrast spec
  1760→    contrast_weights(contrast_spec, term, ...)
  1761→  })
  1762→  
  1763→  # Filter out any NULL results (if any elements weren't contrast_spec)
  1764→  results_list <- results_list[!sapply(results_list, is.null)]
  1765→  
  1766→  # Set the names of the results list based on the names of the contrasts
  1767→  contrast_names <- purrr::map_chr(results_list, "name")
  1768→  results_list <- purrr::set_names(results_list, contrast_names)
  1769→  
  1770→  return(results_list)
  1771→}
